heading: Penalties
label: 6761 Penalties
notes: Delegation of FunctionsFor authority of Secretary of Commerce to carry out certain functions with respect to proceedings under subsec. (a), and to issue regulations with respect thereto, see section 4 of Ex. Ord. No. 13128, , 64 F.R. 34703, set out as a note under .June 25, 1999section 6711 of this title

children.heading: Prohibited acts relating to inspections
children.label: A Prohibited acts relating to inspections
children.text: Any person that is determined, in accordance with paragraph (2), to have violated shall be required by order to pay a civil penalty in an amount not to exceed $25,000 for each such violation. For purposes of this paragraph, each day such a violation of continues shall constitute a separate violation of that section.section 6726 of this titlesection 6726 of this title

children.heading: Recordkeeping violations
children.label: B Recordkeeping violations
children.text: Any person that is determined, in accordance with paragraph (2), to have violated shall be required by order to pay a civil penalty in an amount not to exceed $5,000 for each such violation.section 6745 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before imposing an order described in paragraph (1) against a person under this subsection for a violation of section 6726 or 6745 of this title, the Secretary of State shall provide the person or entity with notice and, upon request made within 15 days of the date of the notice, a hearing respecting the violation.

children.heading: Conduct of hearing
children.label: B Conduct of hearing
children.text: Any hearing so requested shall be conducted before an administrative law judge. The hearing shall be conducted in accordance with the requirements of . If no hearing is so requested, the Secretary of State’s imposition of the order shall constitute a final and unappealable order.section 554 of title 5

children.heading: Issuance of orders
children.label: C Issuance of orders
children.text: If the administrative law judge determines, upon the preponderance of the evidence received, that a person or entity named in the complaint has violated section 6726 or 6745 of this title, the administrative law judge shall state his findings of fact and issue and cause to be served on such person or entity an order described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Factors for determination of penalty amounts
children.label: D Factors for determination of penalty amounts
children.text: In determining the amount of any civil penalty, the administrative law judge shall take into account the nature, circumstances, extent, and gravity of the violation or violations and, with respect to the violator, the ability to pay, effect on ability to continue to do business, any history of prior such violations, the degree of culpability, the existence of an internal compliance program, and such other matters as justice may require.

children.heading: Administrative appellate review
children.label: 3 Administrative appellate review
children.text: The decision and order of an administrative law judge shall become the final agency decision and order of the head of the United States National Authority unless, within 30 days, the head of the United States National Authority modifies or vacates the decision and order, with or without conditions, in which case the decision and order of the head of the United States National Authority shall become a final order under this subsection.

children.heading: Offsets
children.label: 4 Offsets
children.text: The amount of the civil penalty under a final order of the United States National Authority may be deducted from any sums owed by the United States to the person.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 5 Judicial review
children.text: A person adversely affected by a final order respecting an assessment may, within 30 days after the date the final order is issued, file a petition in the Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit or for any other circuit in which the person resides or transacts business.

children.label: A
children.text: after the order making the assessment has become a final order and if such person does not file a petition for judicial review of the order in accordance with paragraph (5), or

children.label: B
children.text: after a court in an action brought under paragraph (5) has entered a final judgment in favor of the United States National Authority,

children.heading: Criminal
children.label: b Criminal
children.text: Any person who knowingly violates any provision of section 6726 or 6745 of this title, shall, in addition to or in lieu of any civil penalty which may be imposed under subsection (a) for such violation, be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than one year, or both.
